zh.lifeSummary: 當Banadicus Gooch於1585年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾斯利布利其時，她的父親Matthew Goche20歲，母親Alice Coney18歲。她於1605年與William Hammond在英國格盧斯特斯爾斯利布利其結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1681年在她的家鄉享年96歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾斯利布利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Banadicus Gooch于1585年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾斯利布利其时，她的父亲Matthew Goche20岁，母亲Alice Coney18岁。她于1605年与William Hammond在英国格盧斯特斯爾斯利布利其结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1681年在她的家乡享年96岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾斯利布利其。

nameMeanings.name: Gooch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Anglia): from the rare Middle English personal name Goche (also found as Joche). It was current in East Anglia from the early 12th to the early 13th centuries as a variant of Anglo-Norman French Go(s)ce, Jo(s)ce, a pet form of Old French Goscelin.
English: alternatively, a nickname from Anglo-Norman French gouge (from Latin gobio), the nominative form of Old French goujon (from Latin gobionem) ‘gudgeon’, perhaps for a gullible person.

Welsh: in southwestern England, possibly an Anglicized form of Welsh coch, goch ‘red(-haired)’, though the sound change is irregular. Compare Gough . It may also be a variant of Cornish and Welsh Couch , with the same meaning.

variants.name: Gough
meanings: Welsh: from coch, goch ‘red(-haired)’. The mutated form goch is normal after a personal name. In England, where the surname occurs from Welsh migration, it may have become hereditary in medieval times but this development was probably much later in Wales. Compare Gooch and Couch , which may sometimes derive from the same source.
Irish (especially Waterford and Dublin): shortened form of   McGough  .

variants.name: Gouge
meanings: English: variant of   Gooch   or   Couch  .

variants.name: Googe
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire and Lincolnshire): variant of   Gooch  .

variants.name: Couch
meanings: English (Cornwall and Devon): nickname from Middle English couch(e) ‘hump, hunch’, used for a hunchback. In Cornwall and Devon it is usually pronounced as Cooch.
English (Cornwall and Devon): variant of   Gooch  .

English (Cornwall and Devon): from Middle English coche, couche ‘bed, couch, tablecloth’ (Old French couche, culche). It could have been a metonymic occupational name for someone who made such items or a nickname for a lazy individual. Alternatively, it could have been applied to someone who produced couchwork, a luxurious embroidery in which gold or silver thread and jewels were sewn into satin or silk robes.

variants.name: Gotch
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh coch, goch ‘red(-haired)’; see Gooch 3.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Banadicus Gooch was born in 1585, in Slimbridge, Gloucestershire, England, her father, Matthew Goche, was 20 and her mother, Alice Coney, was 18. She married William Hammond in 1605, in Slimbridge, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1681, in her hometown, at the age of 96, and was buried in Slimbridge, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Banadicus Gooch est née en 1585, à Slimbridge, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Matthew Goche, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Alice Coney, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé William Hammond en 1605, à Slimbridge, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1681, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 96 ans, et a été enterrée à Slimbridge, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1605 in Slimbridge, Gloucestershire, England William Hammond geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. ja.lifeSummary: Banadicus Goochが1585年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスリンブリッジで生まれた時，父Matthew Gocheは20歳，母Alice Coneyは18歳であった。1605年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスリンブリッジでWilliam Hammondと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1681年に故郷で96歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスリンブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Banadicus Gooch(이)가 1585년 영국 그루스스터실르 슬림브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Matthew Goche(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Coney(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1605년 영국 그루스스터실르 슬림브리지에서 William Hammond(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1681년 그녀의 고향에서 96세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 슬림브리지에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com William Hammond em 1605, em Slimbridge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Banadicus Gooch родилась в 1585 г. в местности слимбридж, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Matthew Goche было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Coney, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Hammond в 1605 г. в местности слимбридж, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1681 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 96 лет и была похоронена в местности слимбридж, глоусестиршир, Англия.

Se casó con William Hammond en 1605, en Slimbridge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1681, en su pueblo, a la edad de 96 años, y fue sepultada en Slimbridge, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
